_notes: 40 VEP questions modelled on real user confusion patterns from Ensembl/ensembl-vep GitHub issues and the Ensembl helpdesk archive public threads. Used for retrieval eval (MRR, top-1, top-5) and answer faithfulness (RAGAS). Categories are roughly balanced and difficulty skews to match real support volume (easy 40%, medium 40%, hard 20%).

questions.question: What is the minimal VEP command to annotate a VCF against the GRCh38 offline cache?
questions.expected_answer_summary: vep -i input.vcf -o out.vcf --cache --dir_cache /path/to/cache --assembly GRCh38

questions.question: Which input formats does VEP accept besides VCF?
questions.expected_answer_summary: VCF (default), HGVS, variant identifiers (rsID), region notation, and SPDI. Use --format to override autodetection.

questions.question: How do I download and install the indexed offline cache for human release 110?
questions.expected_answer_summary: Run INSTALL.pl --AUTO cf --SPECIES homo_sapiens --ASSEMBLY GRCh38 --CACHEDIR ~/.vep and select the indexed cache. Validate with vep --cache --offline --force_overwrite.

questions.question: How do I make VEP annotate variants in regulatory regions, and which consequence terms appear for them?
questions.expected_answer_summary: Add --regulatory. Output gains TF_binding_site_variant and regulatory_region_variant consequences plus Motif_name and Motif_score_change columns when MotifFeature is hit.

questions.question: How do I add ClinVar significance to VEP output using a custom annotation file?
questions.expected_answer_summary: Download ClinVar VCF, tabix-index, then --custom /path/clinvar.vcf.gz,ClinVar,vcf,exact,0,CLNSIG. Adds a ClinVar_CLNSIG column.

real_world_from.pattern: users confuse --plugin ClinVar (does not exist) with --custom clinvar.vcf.gz

questions.question: What is the difference between the Conservation plugin and GERP scores in VEP output, and how do I enable each?
questions.expected_answer_summary: Conservation plugin returns GERP or PhyloP/PhastCons by pointing at a bigWig compara file. GERP alone can be loaded via --custom with a BEDGraph. Two different data files; one is a plugin wrapper, the other is a direct custom annotation.

questions.question: How do I install and run the LoFtool plugin, and where does VEP look for the plugin data file?
questions.expected_answer_summary: Put LoFtool.pm and LoFtool_scores.txt in $HOME/.vep/Plugins (or pass --dir_plugins). Run with --plugin LoFtool.

questions.question: How do I keep only missense variants in VEP output using filter_vep?
questions.expected_answer_summary: filter_vep -i annotated.vcf -o missense.vcf --filter "Consequence is missense_variant"

questions.question: My VEP run is slow on 50M variants offline. Which flags should I combine and what are the tradeoffs?
questions.expected_answer_summary: Combine --fork N (N = number of cores), increase --buffer_size to ~50000-100000, add --no_stats, ensure --offline and --cache, and use --pick if you only need a single consequence per variant. Tradeoff: more forks use more RAM per worker (~1-2 GB), and --pick loses alternative transcript annotations.

questions.question: How do I POST a batch of HGVS notations to the VEP REST API and what is the rate limit?
questions.expected_answer_summary: POST to /vep/{species}/hgvs with body {"hgvs_notations": [...]}. Limit is 200 variants per request. Rate limit is around 15 requests per second per IP.

questions.question: What does SO_term pick_order mean in VEP and when should I override it?
questions.expected_answer_summary: --pick_order controls which fields decide the single representative consequence when --pick is used. Default is canonical,appris,mane_select,mane_plus_clinical,tsl,biotype,ccds,rank,length. Override when you prefer MANE Select or APPRIS over canonical.

questions.question: How do I report HGVS notations in VEP output instead of or alongside the default annotation?
questions.expected_answer_summary: Add --hgvs and point --fasta at the genome FASTA. Output gains HGVSc (coding) and HGVSp (protein) columns.

questions.question: What is the difference between --pick and --flag_pick in VEP?
questions.expected_answer_summary: --pick keeps only the picked consequence line (smaller output). --flag_pick keeps all lines and marks the picked one with PICK=1 (you keep full data).

questions.question: How do I use MANE Select transcripts in VEP?
questions.expected_answer_summary: Add --mane. Output gains MANE_SELECT and MANE_PLUS_CLINICAL columns. GRCh38 only.

questions.question: Why are my VEP annotations missing for some variants even though the VCF parses fine?
questions.expected_answer_summary: Variants far from any gene are assigned intergenic_variant and get fewer annotations. Use --distance to widen the upstream/downstream window (default 5000bp). --nearest symbol reports the nearest gene when there is no overlap.

questions.question: Which species and assemblies are supported in the current VEP cache and how do I list them?
questions.expected_answer_summary: Run INSTALL.pl --AUTO a. Choose from Ensembl vertebrates or Ensembl Genomes divisions (fungi, plants, metazoa, protists, bacteria).

questions.question: How do I annotate with a GFF instead of the cache?
questions.expected_answer_summary: Sort the GFF, bgzip it, tabix-index it, and pass --gff your.gff.gz --fasta your.fa. Skip --cache for this mode.

questions.question: VEP is reporting duplicate consequences for the same variant. Why?
questions.expected_answer_summary: One line per variant per transcript is the default. Variants overlapping multiple transcripts produce multiple lines. Use --pick, --flag_pick, or --most_severe to collapse.

questions.question: How do I add SIFT and PolyPhen scores to VEP output?
questions.expected_answer_summary: --sift b and --polyphen b return both score and prediction. Use s for score only, p for prediction only. Human cache only.

questions.question: What is the --everything flag in VEP and should I use it in production?
questions.expected_answer_summary: --everything turns on a bundle: --sift b --polyphen b --ccds --hgvs --symbol --numbers --domains --regulatory --canonical --protein --biotype --uniprot --tsl --appris --gene_phenotype --af --af_1kg --af_esp --af_gnomADe --af_gnomADg --max_af --pubmed --variant_class. Great for exploration, slower and verbose for production pipelines.

questions.question: Can VEP run on non-human species offline and does --refseq work for them?
questions.expected_answer_summary: Yes, any cached species works offline. --refseq is currently human-only; other species use Ensembl transcripts via --merged or the default Ensembl set.

questions.question: How do I get allele frequencies from gnomAD in VEP output?
questions.expected_answer_summary: --af_gnomADe adds exome gnomAD AFs, --af_gnomADg adds genome gnomAD AFs, --max_af adds MAX_AF across all sources. Human only.

questions.question: How can I output VEP annotations as JSON for downstream tooling?
questions.expected_answer_summary: --json produces one JSON object per variant (NDJSON). Combine with --output_file.

questions.question: How do I run VEP in a Docker container offline?
questions.expected_answer_summary: docker run -v $HOME/.vep:/opt/vep/.vep ensemblorg/ensembl-vep vep -i /data/in.vcf --cache --offline --dir_cache /opt/vep/.vep

questions.question: What is the --merged option and when should I use it?
questions.expected_answer_summary: --merged uses the combined Ensembl+RefSeq cache, adding a SOURCE column to distinguish transcripts. Use when you want both annotation sets in one run. Requires downloading the merged cache.

questions.question: Why does VEP warn about mismatching FASTA and cache versions?
questions.expected_answer_summary: The FASTA must match the cache assembly exactly. Warnings usually mean GRCh37 FASTA with a GRCh38 cache or vice versa. Download the assembly-matched FASTA and re-run. Use --check_ref to catch mismatches early.

questions.question: How do I annotate structural variants with VEP?
questions.expected_answer_summary: VEP handles SV via the VCF SVTYPE and END fields. Consequences reflect transcript overlap and breakpoints. --overlaps adds overlap percentages.

questions.question: Can I run VEP entirely without internet access?
questions.expected_answer_summary: Yes: --offline --cache --dir_cache /path --fasta /path.fa. No database connection is attempted. Some plugins still need their own data files.

questions.question: How do I use the AlphaMissense plugin?
questions.expected_answer_summary: Download AlphaMissense_hg38.tsv.gz, tabix-index with -s 1 -b 2 -e 2. Run --plugin AlphaMissense,file=/path/AlphaMissense_hg38.tsv.gz. Adds am_class and am_pathogenicity columns.

questions.question: How do I resolve HGVSp output that uses Ensembl protein IDs when I need RefSeq NP_ IDs?
questions.expected_answer_summary: Use --refseq (human only) or --merged cache. HGVSp will then reference NP_ identifiers for RefSeq transcripts. For non-human species, post-process by mapping ENSP->NP_ via a local mapping file.

questions.question: My VEP output has empty Consequence fields for some lines. What does this mean?
questions.expected_answer_summary: Empty Consequence usually means VEP could not process the variant: ref-allele mismatch with the assembly, non-ACGT bases, or out-of-range coordinates. Check stderr or the warnings file.

questions.question: How do I control which extra columns appear in VEP text output?
questions.expected_answer_summary: --fields "Uploaded_variation,Location,Consequence,SYMBOL" restricts columns. Works with --tab or CSQ field in --vcf mode.

questions.question: How do I annotate variants with the dbNSFP plugin?
questions.expected_answer_summary: Download dbNSFP, merge per-chromosome files, tabix-index. --plugin dbNSFP,/path/dbNSFP4.4a.gz,REVEL_score,CADD_phred pulls those columns into VEP output.

questions.question: Is there a REST endpoint for a single variant query and how do I call it?
questions.expected_answer_summary: GET /vep/{species}/region/{chr}:{start}-{end}/{allele}?content-type=application/json. Returns a JSON array with one object per transcript consequence.

questions.question: How do I upgrade my VEP cache from one release to the next without redownloading everything?
questions.expected_answer_summary: VEP does not do incremental cache upgrades. Each release ships a complete cache. Keep old caches in parallel directories (e.g. ~/.vep/homo_sapiens/109_GRCh38, 110_GRCh38) and point --dir_cache at the version you want. rsync to mirror the Ensembl FTP for bandwidth savings.

questions.question: How do I make VEP emit only the most severe consequence per variant?
questions.expected_answer_summary: --most_severe produces one line per variant with only the single most severe SO term. It drops per-transcript detail.

questions.question: What is the SpliceAI plugin and what data does it need?
questions.expected_answer_summary: SpliceAI predicts splice-altering probability. Download precomputed SNV VCFs (hg19 or hg38) from Illumina, tabix-index. --plugin SpliceAI,snv=path/spliceai.snv.vcf.gz,indel=path/spliceai.indel.vcf.gz. Output columns start with SpliceAI_pred_.

questions.question: Why do VEP results differ between the web interface and my local offline run?
questions.expected_answer_summary: The web UI runs the latest Ensembl release with --everything style options on live databases. Local offline runs use whatever cache you downloaded and only the options you passed. Mismatches usually come from cache version drift or differing option sets. Pin cache version and match web options with --everything to compare.

questions.question: How do I filter VEP output to keep only variants with CADD_PHRED > 20?
questions.expected_answer_summary: filter_vep -i annotated.vcf -o high_cadd.vcf --filter "CADD_PHRED > 20". Requires the CADD plugin to be enabled during annotation.

questions.question: What is the recommended way to cite VEP in a publication?
questions.expected_answer_summary: Cite McLaren et al., 'The Ensembl Variant Effect Predictor', Genome Biology 2016 17:122. Also reference the specific Ensembl release used.
